message:
fix(fcp-sdk): require opt-in for ambient FCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS override (br-3a3r6)

P2 — fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs::ConnectorRuntimeConfig::from_manifest
previously read FCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS unconditionally and let the env
silently override the manifest's pinned [timeouts] section. The
manifest is operator-pinned (and will be signed in a future regime),
the env is ambient and writable by anything in the connector's process
tree — that ordering inverts the trust hierarchy.

Defense-in-depth pattern shared with br-n4429 (registry trust roots):
require an explicit `FCP_ALLOW_AMBIENT_TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE=1` env opt-in
before the env override is honored. Without the opt-in:
- FCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS is not read (the manifest always wins),
- a malformed FCP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS cannot cause a startup error or
  be used as a denial-of-service vector since the value is never
  consulted.

Implementation:
- New `ALLOW_AMBIENT_TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE_ENV_VAR` const + pure
  `opt_in_value_allows_override` classifier (case-sensitive exact-match
  against "1" / "true" / "yes" so a typo like "True" or " 1" can't
  accidentally enable the gate).
- `from_manifest` now consults the gate before reading the timeout env.
- The lower-level `from_manifest_with_request_timeout_override` still
  applies whatever override is handed to it — that's the explicit-opt-in
  API surface intentional callers use.

Tests:
- Crate has #![forbid(unsafe_code)] so std::env::set_var (unsafe in
  Rust 2024) is not usable. Added thread-local test-only override
  pattern (TEST_*_VALUE) with RAII guards (OptInOverrideGuard /
  RequestTimeoutOverrideGuard) — same pattern as the
  TEST_REGISTRY_TRUST_ROOTS_OVERRIDE shipped under br-n4429.
- 5 new tests pin: manifest-wins-without-opt-in, env-honored-with-opt-in,
  invalid-env-with-opt-in-errors, invalid-env-without-opt-in-ignored,
  truthy-value-recognition.
- Updated config_from_manifest_str_parses_embedded_manifest to assert
  manifest precedence (was racing on ambient env state from the test
  shell).

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-sdk --lib → 1006/1006 pass (126 of which
are migration::* including the 5 new ones).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
